facial.direction_interpretation: Higher values indicate stronger, more frequent smiling.
facial.tooltip: Mean intensity of smile-related action units (AU06 cheek raiser + AU12 lip-corner puller).

facial.direction_interpretation: Higher values indicate more variable / dynamic smiling over time.
facial.tooltip: Temporal variability (standard deviation) of smile-related action units (AU06 + AU12).

facial.direction_interpretation: Higher values indicate more brow lowering / negative facial affect.
facial.tooltip: Mean intensity of AU04 (brow lowerer), a marker of negative or concentrated affect.

facial.direction_interpretation: Higher values indicate more variable brow-lowering activity over time.
facial.tooltip: Temporal variability (standard deviation) of AU04 (brow lowerer).

facial.direction_interpretation: Higher values indicate greater overall facial expressivity.
facial.tooltip: Mean intensity across all available action units.

facial.direction_interpretation: Higher values indicate more variable overall facial expressivity over time.
facial.tooltip: Mean temporal variability (standard deviation) across all available action units.

gaze.direction_interpretation: Higher values indicate more visual attention to the screen / stimulus.
gaze.tooltip: Proportion of valid gaze frames directed at the display region.

gaze.direction_interpretation: Higher values indicate more gaze aversion / looking away from the screen.
gaze.tooltip: Proportion of valid gaze frames directed away from the display region.

gaze.direction_interpretation: Higher values indicate more horizontal eye-movement / scanning.
gaze.tooltip: Standard deviation of the horizontal gaze angle.

gaze.direction_interpretation: Higher values indicate more vertical eye-movement / scanning.
gaze.tooltip: Standard deviation of the vertical gaze angle.

voice.direction_interpretation: Higher values indicate more pitch modulation / melodic speech.
voice.tooltip: F0 (pitch) variability, percentile range 0-2 in semitones from 27.5 Hz.

voice.direction_interpretation: Higher values indicate more dynamic / variable loudness.

voice.tooltip: Voiced segments per second (proxy for speaking rate).

voice.direction_interpretation: Higher values indicate more variable speaking rate across topics.
voice.tooltip: Variability of voiced segments per second across SIT phases.

head.direction_interpretation: Higher values indicate more vertical head movement (nodding).
head.tooltip: Amount of vertical head-pose movement (standard deviation of head pitch).

head.direction_interpretation: Higher values indicate more variable vertical head movement.
head.tooltip: Spread of vertical head-pose movement (inter-quartile range of head pitch).

head.direction_interpretation: Higher values indicate more horizontal head movement (head shaking / turning).
head.tooltip: Amount of lateral / yaw head movement (standard deviation of head yaw).

head.direction_interpretation: Higher values indicate more variable horizontal head movement.
head.tooltip: Spread of lateral / yaw head movement (inter-quartile range of head yaw).

mimicry.direction_interpretation: Higher values indicate stronger temporal alignment of smiling with the partner.
mimicry.tooltip: Maximum lagged cross-correlation of smile action units with the interaction partner.

mimicry.direction_interpretation: Higher values indicate more similar smile expression patterns to the partner.
mimicry.tooltip: Maximum lag-aware cosine similarity of smile action-unit trajectories with the partner.

mimicry.direction_interpretation: Larger values indicate a longer delay before mirroring the partner's smile.
mimicry.tooltip: Lag (in frames) at which the smile cross-correlation peaks.

mimicry.direction_interpretation: Higher values indicate less similar expression dynamics to the partner.
mimicry.tooltip: Length-normalized dynamic-time-warping distance between participant and partner AU trajectories.

mimicry.direction_interpretation: Higher values indicate stronger directed temporal dependency on the partner.
mimicry.tooltip: Granger-causality-based directed dependency from partner to participant.
